# Semantica Evidence Boundary

## Map

| Need | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| What RAWR relies on | Semantica as optional evidence discovery/source-span aid |
| Current lab evidence | Read-only branch/source-mining context only |
| System impact | Review targeting and source discovery, not proof promotion |
| Proof ceiling | Semantica output is never architecture authority or runtime proof |

## Current Boundary

- The `codex/semantica-first-pipeline-implementation` branch is inspected
  read-only as evidence discovery only.
- That branch diverges from the runtime lab stack and must not be merged into
  this implementation branch.
- Semantica output may point reviewers to source spans, but it is not
  architecture authority and cannot promote a manifest entry to proof without a
  concrete gate.

## Not Proven

Semantica evidence does not prove runtime behavior, Lab-Production Proof,
architecture truth, vendor fidelity, or proof-category promotion.

## Future Use Rule

Use Semantica as an evidence adapter alongside `rg`, `find`, topology checks,
and reviewer judgment. Do not use generated findings, suggestions, graph
entities, or claim rows as promotable proof unless a separate validator and
runtime lab gate establish the claim.

No vendor-doc pass is needed for passive source discovery. Any future
Semantica integration, modeling, or generated-claim workflow requires a focused
official-doc/source review before it becomes normative guidance.
